attacking_style: Launches decisive attacks 2-4km from summit finishes, particularly on final climbs. Favors late-stage opportunism from breakaways over sustained GC dominance efforts. Shows excellent tactical timing but occasionally misjudges finish gradients (700m too early at steep finishes).
team_tactics: Operates with minimal domestique support in decisive moments—often isolated or personally chasing threats rather than delegating. INEOS frequently relegates him to policing/pacing duties despite GC credentials. Movistar historically gave more autonomy but conservative tempo control rather than aggressive setups.
terrain_preference: Excels on sustained 2-4km summit finishes with moderate gradients. Vulnerable on time trials (lost 1:25, 1:44 in decisive ITTs) and technical wet descents (crashed in Catalunya). Cold/altitude conditions unexpectedly compromise performance despite historical wet-weather reputation.
vulnerability: ITT weakness repeatedly costs podiums (1:25+ losses in decisive efforts). Catastrophic single-stage collapses when form falters (2:56, 1:25 losses). Cannot match Pogačar/Roglič in pure sprint finishes when equally positioned. Crashes on technical descents expose handling limitations.
key_patterns: Attacks 2-4km from summit finishes across multiple Grand Tours, timing consistent across years
Prioritizes KOM/stage hunting over GC when outgunned, switched tactics mid-Vuelta 2022 successfully
Isolated in decisive moments across teams, personally chases threats without domestique support observed 4+ times
ITT losses exceed 1 minute in crucial stages, cost Giro 2022 podium and Tour 2021 position
Late-race form peaks in third week (Vuelta 2020 Stage 17, attacked when rivals faded)
Opportunistic breakaway stage wins from reduced groups, 5+ victories via this pattern since 2018
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 showed declining results—dropped before Monument selections (Lombardia 8th), went backwards on climbs with teammates attacking (Catalunya). Brief Dauphiné resurgence couldn't be sustained. Peak Grand Tour competitiveness appears 2019-2022.
